steps.title: Check current n8n deployment
steps.description:
Verify your n8n version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
helm list -n n8n 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working n8n installation.

steps.title: Review n8n configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant n8n configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get configmap -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=n8n
```
pdf-parse looks like complete unsupported and it use an old pdf.js version.

A maintained alternative of pdf-parse should used with newer pdf.js.

Currently i'm look into it, if we just can use pdf.js without wrapper.

steps.title: Apply the fix for replace pdf-parse
steps.description:
Hey ,

That is neat, is it published to the community node repo?

I am also going to mark this one as closed for now as it isn't a bug as such and the PR was closed out with the intention of us rebuilding the node in the future.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get events -n n8n --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "replace pdf-parse" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hey ,

That is neat, is it published to the community node repo?

I am also going to mark this one as closed for now as it isn't a bug as such and the PR was closed out with the intention of us rebuilding the node in the future.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with n8n installed or the issue environment reproducible.
